div.head: The Minister in Finland (Schoenfeld) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
389. With reference to a report which I understand appeared recently in the New York Post and perhaps in other American newspapers
to the effect that direct negotiations between Finland and the Soviet Union for an armistice were contemplated, I learn from Secretary General of Foreign Office that nothing is known here of any such negotiations. He is inclined to think that this report may be related to a recent Russian broadcast in which it is reported to have been said that Finland would be well-advised to consider such a step.

Any such report should in my opinion be judged in the light of Finnish Government’s consistent statements beginning with President Ryti’s radio address June 26 that Finland’s objectives in present hostilities are limited, and in the light of the fact that this Government’s freedom of action will largely depend upon the attitude of its German co-belligerent.
